body.title: Uncertainty-gated recall in activate.py — drill to episode level when confidence is low
body.result: recall() in activate.py: top-K fetch, mean_score confidence gate, trace expansion on low confidence; 4 tests green
body.description:
activate.py fetches memories by cosine similarity alone. xMemory names this the missing piece: 'similarity is a candidate-generation signal; uncertainty is a decision signal.' When the top-K semantic matches are high-confidence (mean similarity > IGOR_RECALL_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD, default 0.6), return them as-is. When confidence is low, expand: additionally query clan.traces for turns that co-activated the matched memory nodes, and include those trace rows as episode-level context alongside the memory results.

Implementation:
1. After the existing top-K similarity fetch in activate.py, compute mean activation_score of returned nodes.
2. If mean < threshold (env IGOR_RECALL_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD, default 0.6): query clan.traces WHERE nodes @> jsonb_build_array(jsonb_build_object('node_id', m.id)) for matched memory ids, LIMIT 5 most recent per node.
3. Return both memory nodes AND trace rows (or empty trace list on failure) — callers get richer context when needed, lean context when not.
4. Log whether expansion was triggered (for observability).
Non-blocking: if traces query fails, log and return memory-only result.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/activate.py
**Design rules:** non-blocking on trace query failure; configurable threshold via env; no new DB tables — uses existing clan.traces and clan.memories
**Scope boundary:** In: confidence check, conditional trace expansion, log, returned trace rows. Out: changing consolidation, changing how callers use activate, modifying clan.traces schema.
**Test plan:** activate() with high-similarity match (score > 0.6) returns traces=[]. activate() with low-similarity match triggers trace query; result contains trace rows for those nodes. Trace query failure returns memory-only result without raising.
